purpose: The commit fixes a queuing issue in the HCI (Host Controller Interface) for Bluetooth. It ensures that when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, the hci_cmd_sync_queue function returns an error, preventing potential use-after-free (UAF) bugs. This change seems to address a specific problem related to device registration and command handling.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement for asm/termios.h in the n_hdlc.c file. The change seems to be related to code cleanup and dependency management.
The commit updates documentation for a store metric 'N/A' in the perf c2c tool. It seems to be a minor change to reflect new information in the report table.
The commit fixes issues related to vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping, specifically addressing race conditions and ensuring proper synchronization.
The commit adds documentation for the rt5682s codec, specifically for the AVDD and MICVDD supplies. It seems to be a minor update to the device tree binding.

The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding a missing iounmap() call. This is a bug fix, as the error handling path in the probe function releases a resource that is not freed in the remove function.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct, which is a minor change to clean up the code.

The commit changes the type of a struct member 'key_idx' from unsigned to signed to avoid warnings from static checkers. This is done because in some cases, the value -1 is assigned to it when the key is NULL.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in the RISC-V defconfigs. It does not change the functionality but improves the configuration file's readability and maintainability.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode in the mcp251xfd driver. This improves code readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies selftests for RTC (Real-Time Clock) to skip tests when RTC is not present. This change ensures that tests do not fail unnecessarily when the RTC is not available.

The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in the 'midi.c' file. This change is made to clean up a clang scan build warning, indicating that the value stored in 'bytes' is never actually read.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing rcuscale to be built without RCU Tasks. This change aims to reduce kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes duplicated text 'be split' in a header file spte.h related to KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) x86/mmu.

The commit reverts a change to the clk table population for dcn315 based on dcfclk. This was done to prevent underflow situations that arose due to how pmfw fills out the table when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU. It seems to be a device tree binding update, which is a technical change to support a specific hardware configuration.

The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp to make it consistent with other names in the DW PCIe core driver. This change does not add new functionality but rather improves the naming convention.
The commit fixes a warning related to format specifiers in a printf-like function. It changes the format specifier to match the type of the argument being passed.

The commit fixes a branch range check issue for MIPS architecture. It casts the upper bound of the branch range to long to perform a signed comparison, preventing a negative offset warning. The change seems to be a bug fix, improving the handling of branch ranges in the context of jump labels.
The commit removes a case for HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD from SetHwReg8188EU() and directly calls a function instead. This is part of an effort to get rid of unwanted hal layer code.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel. It addresses sparse warnings related to undeclared symbols, specifically in the context of CPU errata handling.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings.
The commit allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref in preparation for allowing drivers to change the array size. It modifies the v4l2-ctrls functionality.
The commit adds support for new SoCs and PMICs in the socinfo driver. It seems to be an extension of existing functionality rather than a fix or a completely new feature.
The commit enhances the recognition of chip cuts for the rtl8xxxu wifi driver, extending support beyond the previously known versions.

The commit removes custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks, as the core already keeps a software enable count. This change seems to be related to clock management and is likely intended to simplify or improve the existing functionality.
The commit aims to reduce the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by grouping related definitions together. It seems to be a code refactoring effort to improve maintainability and readability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to add functioning I2C nodes later. It seems to be a preparatory change to allow for additional functionality.

The commit fixes comments and typos in the thermal driver for rzg2l. It replaces 'Capture times' with 'Total number of ADC data samples', fixes a typo in 'caliberation' to 'calibration', and adjusts coding style for /* comments.
The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the igc driver, which occurs when the HW semaphore cannot be acquired, typically due to surprise removal of the hardware. The fix logs an error and quits.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, specifically changing the clocking style to be more contemporary.
The commit removes a piece of code that checks for IS_SWAPFILE, which is always defined. This suggests that the code being removed is not necessary or is redundant.

The commit implements GTP echo response, which is a feature that allows the system to respond to GTP echo requests. This is done by adding a new functionality to the GTP device, which can receive and respond to GTP echo requests.

The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, improving the effectiveness of cpumask operations. It also introduces cpumask_first_zero() to handle a specific case.

The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR, which was failing and causing a message to be displayed on the console every time a STA roams. The change ensures that the host driver no longer tries to get TIM information from the firmware.

The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier, specifically for the alc5682 headset codec. It introduces a new board configuration, adl_rt5682, and connects the codec to SSP0, following Intel's BT offload design.
The commit adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg requests for protocols that do not support it. This is a technical change to handle zerocopy sendmsg requests properly.
The commit checks if xeCJK.sty exists before loading it to prevent errors on certain Linux distributions.
The commit allows 'case' macros in checkpatch.pl, modifying error reporting behavior for certain in-kernel driver usage.

The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in drm/simpledrm. It aims to improve the architecture and allow for easier code sharing among drivers. The changes seem to be primarily technical and do not add new functionality.

The commit adds a JPEG decoding worker interface to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195. This suggests that the primary purpose is to enhance the functionality of the media component, specifically for JPEG decoding.
The commit removes pointless VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() as the information is already covered by tracepoints and scsi tracing.

The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in the drm/amd/display module. It allows the system to enter a low-power state (zstate) when the display is in a freesync state and there is extra time available in the vblank period. The commit triggers a full update to calculate the time gained from extra vblank lines and allows zstate entry if the time gained is greater than 5 ms.

The commit fixes a type mismatch issue in the hqd_destroy() callback function, which was causing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure. The change updates the function pointer prototype to match the reality, ensuring that the third parameter, reset_type, has the correct type of 'uint32_t'.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in the dt-bindings for pinctrl in stm32. It also adds a missing 'additionalProperties' configuration.

The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver for GCC 12. It silences the warning by changing how the compiler handles certain operations, but does not fix any bug or add new functionality.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto code. This change aims to simplify the code.

The commit reorganizes and refines the code related to fixed file installation and removal within the io_uring framework. It moves these functionalities closer to the filetable code, which seems to be a more appropriate place for them. The change involves modifying helpers to take a ctx (context) rather than an io_kiocb, making the code more aligned with its purpose.
The commit updates device tree bindings to support separate nodes for G1 and G2 decoders, which are independent blocks sharing a power domain controller.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and combines its contents into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro. This change simplifies the code by reducing the number of macros.
